lines: # Renders the rollup to the run's Summary tab (##vso[task.uploadsummary]).
-# The Vally eval harness ships hand-written ES modules under lib/ (glob.js, verdict.js).
+# The Vally eval harness ships hand-written ES modules under lib/ (glob.ts, verdict.ts).
# Most repos ignore a top-level `lib/` (Python build output); re-include ours here so the
# harness stays tracked wherever eng/common is synced (else node fails ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND).
# Living in eng/common means this fix travels with the sync to every language SDK repo, so no
-This folder holds the JavaScript glue the Vally eval CI runs (matrix sharding, the shard
+This folder holds the TypeScript glue the Vally eval CI runs (matrix sharding, the shard
runner, the JUnit summary) **and** pins the [`@microsoft/vally-cli`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@microsoft/vally-cli)
version those shards install. The CLI and its full transitive dependency tree are locked by
the committed `package-lock.json` instead of resolved fresh from semver ranges on every run.
- The only dependency should be `@microsoft/vally-cli`, pinned to the version CI should evaluate with.
- `package-lock.json` must be committed so `npm ci` is deterministic.
+The `*.ts` sources run directly through Node's native type stripping (erasable syntax only —
+no `enum`/`namespace`/parameter properties, no emit). CI pins Node `22.x`, which strips types
+unflagged on `>=22.18`; the pipeline `node` invocations and the `npm test` script pass
+`--experimental-strip-types` so the same sources also run on older local Node (`>=22.6`), which
+prints a harmless `ExperimentalWarning`. Relative imports use explicit `.ts` specifiers, as Node requires.
+  @ `ef7dd74c13aa9ca12b67b33b9dc4b5d1419a46f0`) and ported to TypeScript. It lives here under
+  `eng/common` (rather than the specs repo's `.github/shared` path) so it travels with the
+  eng/common sync into the language repos. Re-vendor from upstream rather than editing locally;
+  to share these primitives instead of copying.
// Maps a JUnit file path back to its shard and job attempt. Result artifacts download into
// folders named `eval-result-<shardName>-<attempt>` (the attempt suffix keeps "Rerun failed
// Fallback patterns when run with no --pattern. Mirrors archetype-eval.yml's evalGlobs default.
// The pinned Vally CLI is installed next to this script (package.json + node_modules live here),
// so the npm --prefix is just this file's own directory.
@@ -30,6 +30,9 @@ export function runShard({ evalArgs, shardName, outputDir, threshold = 0.8 }) {
// Do NOT abort on a non-zero exit — the verdict below is authoritative.
+  // Uses spawnSync (not the vendored execFile helper) on purpose: the shard needs vally's output
+  // streamed live to the log (stdio: "inherit") and must keep going on a non-zero exit, whereas
+  // the exec helper captures output and rejects on failure.
+// Secure process/exec primitives (execFile wrapper with logging + a larger default maxBuffer).
+//   @ ef7dd74c13aa9ca12b67b33b9dc4b5d1419a46f0 and ported to TypeScript (erasable syntax only,
+//   run via Node native type stripping). See azure-sdk-tools#16296 for the plan to share these
+//   primitives properly instead of copying. Kept under eng/common/scripts/eval/lib so it travels
+//   with the eng/common sync into the language repos (unlike .github/shared, which does not sync);
+//   this is why the folder path differs from the specs repo.
+// Minimal logger contract used by the exec helpers (subset of the specs repo ILogger).
+  /** Max bytes allowed on stdout or stderr. Default: 16 * 1024 * 1024. */
+  /** Prefix to pass to npm via "--prefix". */
+export type ExecError = Error & { stdout?: string; stderr?: string; code?: number };
+ * Checks whether an unknown error object is an ExecError.
+export function isExecError(error: unknown): error is ExecError {
+ * Wraps `child_process.execFile()`, adding logging and a larger default maxBuffer.
+    // Node default is 1024 * 1024, which is too small for some git commands returning many
+    // entities or large file content. To support "git show", should be larger than the largest
+    // swagger file in the repo (2.5 MB as of 2/28/2025).
+    // execFile(file, args) is more secure than exec(cmd), since the latter is vulnerable to
+ * Calls `execFile()` with appropriate arguments to run `npm` on all platforms.
+export async function execNpm(args: string[], options: ExecNpmOptions = {}): Promise<ExecResult> {
+          // Only way I could find to run "npm" on Windows, without using the shell (e.g.
+          // The "--" MUST come BEFORE "npm-cli.js", to ensure args are sent to the script
+          // unchanged. If the "--" comes after "npm-cli.js", the args sent to the script will be
+          // ["--", ...args], which is NOT equivalent, and can break if args itself contains
+export async function execNpmExec(args: string[], options: ExecNpmOptions = {}): Promise<ExecResult> {
// Formats a 0..1 ratio as a fixed-1-decimal percentage string (e.g. 97.1).

"description": "Vally eval CI glue scripts (matrix sharding, shard runner, JUnit summary) plus the pinned @microsoft/vally-cli the shards install from the committed lockfile. Synced via eng/common; not published.",
// fetch + checkout. Primes the cache Vally's `environment.git` fixtures point at; FETCH_HEAD
// is landed on a local branch named <ref> so `git worktree add --detach <tmp> <ref>` resolves.
-// Throws on non-zero git exit so a failed clone stops immediately.
-// NOTE: azure-rest-api-specs ships shared exec primitives (.github/shared/src/exec.js), but
-// they aren't importable here — eng/common has no cross-repo dependency on that package and
-// those helpers carry specs-repo-specific assumptions. This intentionally stays a tiny local
-// wrapper; revisit if a shared eng/common exec utility is ever introduced.
+// Throws on non-zero git exit so a failed clone stops immediately. Delegates to the shared
+// execFile helper (copied from azure-rest-api-specs; see lib/exec.ts), which runs git without a
+// shell (no injection) and captures output; git's stderr is surfaced only when the command fails.
* @param {number} [options.maxAgeHours] Skip the refresh fetch if cached within this window.
// init + fetch <ref> (not clone --depth 1) so any branch/tag/SHA is pinned on a cold cache.
// Land FETCH_HEAD on a real local branch named <ref> (not detached) so Vally's worktree
// Re-point the local <ref> branch at FETCH_HEAD (also repairs a previously detached cache)
// so the worktree fixtures keep resolving.
-    console.log(cache); // echo the cache path so a wrapper can capture it
+      console.log(cache); // echo the cache path so a wrapper can capture it
-// node:test unit tests for build-eval-summary.js (port of Build-EvalSummary.Tests.ps1).
+// node:test unit tests for build-eval-summary.ts (port of Build-EvalSummary.Tests.ps1).
import { after, before, beforeEach, describe, it } from "node:test";
// Convenience: build the summary and also write the markdown, returning both.
-// node:test unit tests for collect-stimuli.js (port of Split-EvalSuite.Tests.ps1).
+// node:test unit tests for collect-stimuli.ts (port of Split-EvalSuite.Tests.ps1).
// Sharding is always by `area` tag: one shard per area, each carrying every eval of that
import { after, before, describe, it } from "node:test";
// Helper: write a file, creating parent directories as needed.
-// Tests for init-eval-git-fixtures.js — discovery only (the dry-run path that clones nothing).
+// Tests for init-eval-git-fixtures.ts — discovery only (the dry-run path that clones nothing).
import { after, before, describe, it } from "node:test";
-// node:test unit tests for lib/verdict.js (port of Invoke-EvalShard.Tests.ps1, which
+// node:test unit tests for lib/verdict.ts (port of Invoke-EvalShard.Tests.ps1, which
